perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not need to be marked as such and is defined as any and all technical, business financial and other information belonging to either party, including but not limited to products, descriptions, drawings, bills of material, assembly drawings, compositions, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, know how, inventions, manufacturing techniques and processes, marketing and sales processes and techniques, customer lists, price lists, suppliers, current and future product developments.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Data does not need to be marked as such and is defined as any and all technical, business financial and other information belonging to either party, including but not limited to products, descriptions, drawings, bills of material, assembly drawings, compositions, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, know how, inventions, manufacturing techniques and processes, marketing and sales processes and techniques, customer lists, price lists, suppliers, current and future product developments.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' only in this definition creates ambiguity. The rest of the document refers to 'Confidential Information.' This raises questions of whether the obligations concerning 'Confidential Information' also apply to the redefined 'Proprietary Data', creating an In-Text contradiction with the rest of the document, where 'Confidential Information' is used extensively and is never defined again.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Confidential Information may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the definition, while the contradicted text and the rest of the document still refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction because it is unclear whether the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' also apply to the newly defined 'Proprietary Data'. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology which leads to in-text contradictions, because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, especially considering that 'Confidential Information' is never defined again and used in the rest of the document.

perturbation.original_text: All information disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information whether or not it is so marked. It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: All data disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information whether or not it is so marked. It is the intent of the Entities to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' makes it unclear if all types of 'information' are covered or only 'data,' which could be interpreted more narrowly. Earlier in Section 1 'Parties' was also changed to 'Entities', this then creates a contradiction due to the previous perturbation. This also potentially limits the scope of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information does not need to be marked as such and is defined as any and all technical, business financial and other information belonging to either party, including but not limited to products, descriptions, drawings, bills of material, assembly drawings, compositions, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, know how, inventions, manufacturing techniques and processes, marketing and sales processes and techniques, customer lists, price lists, suppliers, current and future product developments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'information' with 'data', potentially narrowing the scope of what is considered Confidential Information. The Contradicted Text defines Confidential Information as 'any and all technical, business financial and other information'. If 'data' is interpreted more narrowly than 'information', it creates a contradiction because the original intent was to protect all types of information. The changed text makes it unclear if all types of 'information' are still covered, leading to a conflict with the broad definition provided in the Contradicted Text. The change from 'Parties' to 'Entities' also supports the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Confidential Information may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Sensitive Data and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Sensitive Data. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Sensitive Data may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' creates a contradiction since 'Confidential Information' is used in the agreement extensively, and a redefinition this late in the document creates uncertainity and opens the agreement up for misinterpretation. As Section 1 defines the term differently and this term is not defined again. This affects the scope of what needs to be returned
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information does not need to be marked as such and is defined as any and all technical, business financial and other information belonging to either party, including but not limited to products, descriptions, drawings, bills of material, assembly drawings, compositions, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, know how, inventions, manufacturing techniques and processes, marketing and sales processes and techniques, customer lists, price lists, suppliers, current and future product developments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' extensively. Because the changed text does not define 'Sensitive Data', and 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined elsewhere in the document, the change creates a contradiction. It introduces uncertainty about whether the existing definition of 'Confidential Information' applies to the new term 'Sensitive Data,' or whether 'Sensitive Data' refers to a different set of information entirely. The Perturbation Explanation states this substitution late in the document creates uncertainity, and opens the agreement up for misinterpretation. Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction: Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Limitation on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Restriction on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial data to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' only in this section again creates confusion. Throughout the document, 'Confidential Information' encompasses a wide range of information. By narrowing it to 'data' here, it suggests that the *use* restriction only applies to data, and not other types of information, such as trade secrets or know-how that are defined as Confidential Information in Section 1. This change contradicts the broader definition of Confidential Information in Section 1. And creates an In-Text contradiction as the 'Limitation on Use' has been changed to a 'Restriction on Use'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information does not need to be marked as such and is defined as any and all technical, business financial and other information belonging to either party, including but not limited to products, descriptions, drawings, bills of material, assembly drawings, compositions, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, know how, inventions, manufacturing techniques and processes, marketing and sales processes and techniques, customer lists, price lists, suppliers, current and future product developments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term 'information' to 'data' within the 'Restriction on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information' section. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' as encompassing 'any and all technical, business financial and other information'. The explanation points out that by narrowing 'Confidential Information' to 'data' in the changed text, it contradicts the broader definition in the contradicted text (Section 1), creating an in-text contradiction. The change in terminology directly limits the scope of the restriction compared to the definition of confidential information, therefore, it is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Entities agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Entities agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Parties' to 'Entities' in this section, while inconsistent with the rest of the document where 'Parties' is predominantly used, introduces a contradiction given the earlier perturbation in Section 1, where 'Parties' was changed to 'Entities' once. Now, with the same change here and the prior contradiction, questions arise regarding the binding nature of this clause, given that it affects 'Entities' while obligations in other parts of the agreement are stated to bind the 'Parties'. This intext contradiction makes the use of 'Parties' used in the agreement and the use of the 'Entities' now in this section creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence when executed and continue for a period of one (1) year. The Parties agree that from the date of first receipt, and for a period of three (3) years following the last disclosure of Confidential Information, the party receiving the Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”) shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any other person, or entity, or use for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement and shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of such information as it does for its own confidential information which it does not desire to have published or disseminated. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of the Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Parties' to 'Entities' in the Changed Text, when considered alongside the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to 'Parties', creates a contradiction. The 'Perturbation Explanation' highlights that this introduces ambiguity regarding which entity is bound by which clause. Specifically, the Contradicted Text states an obligation of 'the Parties', while the changed section now speaks of 'Entities'. If these terms are not explicitly defined as interchangeable, then the Contradicted Text no longer clearly applies to the 'Entities' mentioned in the changed section, creating an in-text contradiction under Misaligned Terminology.
